Off Calendar Aug-19-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Deonte Walker
Off Calendar Aug-19-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. MICHAEL RAIFSNIDER, Esq. (Tel: 415-398-9001) for defendant Alejandro Alvarez (limited scope representation);
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ALEJANDRO ALVAREZ's Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Deonte Walker.

Defendant Alejandro Alvarez stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for defendant (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter discussed and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS.

ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT WILL ENTER THE FOLLOWING ORDER:
(1) WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF WILL SERVE DEFENDANT WITH FULL AND COMPLETE CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION TO EACH OF THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND FORM INTERROGATORIES PREVIOUSLY SERVED UPON HIM.
(2) WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF WILL SERVE DEFENDANT WITHOUT OBJECTION CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION PREVIOUSLY SERVED UPON HIM.
(3) THE COURT WILL RETAIN JURISDICTION OF THIS MATTER AND IF PLAINTIFF FAILS TO COMPLY WITH THE PROVISIONS OF PARAGRAPH (1) & (2) WITHIN TIME PERIOD SPECIFIED, THE COURT WILL GRANT DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS UPON THE SUBMISSION BY DEFENDANT OF AN ORDER DISMISSING WITH PREJUDICE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
(4) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE NO MONETARY SANCTIONS AS THE AMOUNT SOUGHT WAS UNSUPPORTED BY THE RECORD.
(5) IN ADDITION TO SERVING A COPY OF THIS ORDER BY US MAIL TO PLAINTIFF, A COPY SHOULD BE EMAILED TO HIM AT simply.janae@icloud.com as requested in Exhibit 9 in the Defendant's declaration.

DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THE RULING, SUBMIT AT COPY TO PLAINTIFF AT THE ADDRESSES NOTED ABOVE FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY IN WORD FORM TO THE COURT (rennepar85@gmail.com).

(TAMARA REED appeared late via Zoom videoconference with/for plaintiff DEONTE WALKER after the matter was called. The Court informed Ms. Reed and the plaintiff of the outcome of the hearing.)

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Deonte Walker
Off Calendar May-26-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Defendant ALEJANDRO ALVAREZ (Tel: 415-310-6366) in pro per;
Also present: Atty. MICHAEL RAIFSNIDER, Esq. of Raifsnider Law Office (Tel: 415-398-9001).

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ALEJANDRO ALVAREZ's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Deonte Walker

Defendant Mr. Alvarez stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and defendant Mr. Alvarez (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation on their behalf.

The Court confirms with Mr. Raifsnider that he does not have a limited-scope representation on file as to defendant Mr. Alvarez, and, as such, the Court will need to address Mr. Alvarez directly as to today's matter.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court rules as follows: Defendant's motion will be treated as a motion to compel responses to special interrogatories (not as a motion to compel FURTHER responses) and, as such, is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to provide code compliance responses without objections by June 23, 2022. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied without prejudice. If plaintiff does not comply with the court's order, defendant may then file the appropriate motion to enforce the court's order.

Defendant to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Ntc Of Mtn To Set Aside Dismissal
Off Calendar Feb-02-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tamara Reed, (simply.janae@icloud.com) speaking on behalf of pro per Plaintiff, Deonte Walker, (707.689.2931) who is also present in court.
No other appearances.

Discussed, not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DEONTE WALKER'S Motion To Set Aside Dismissal.

Plaintiff's motion to vacate dismissal is granted. Even though plaintiff did not serve the motion in compliance with CCP 1005, defendant waived the defect by addressing the merits of the motion. (See Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697 ["It is well settled that the appearance of a party at the hearing of a motion and his or her opposition to the motion on its merits is a waiver of any defects or irregularities in the notice of motion. This rule applies even when no notice was given at all."].) In any event, plaintiff is entitled to relief from the dismissal because he in fact complied with the court's order of September 10, 2021 and filed the amended complaint by September 24, 2021. Defendant shall respond to the amended complaint by March 4, 2022.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Plaintiff did not timly contest the Court's tenative ruling. Plaintiffs Notice of Appearance was emailed to the court late. Court allows testimony from plaintiff.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral testimony adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

PLAINTIFF DEONTE WALKER'S Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment (Dismissal).

Plaintiff's "motion to set aside default judgment" is denied, as no default judgment was ever entered in this case. Rather, defendant's "motion to dismiss complaint for plaintiff's failure to comply with court's order to file an amended complaint" was granted by an order filed September 24, 2021. Order is signed.
